symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden severe epigastric pain radiating straight through to the back

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Boring epigastric-to-back pain is a hallmark of pancreatitis.
Sudden severe upper-abdominal pain is also consistent with perforation — cannot be excluded remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain relieved by sitting up and leaning forward, worse lying flat

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Postural relief on leaning forward is characteristic of retroperitoneal pancreatic inflammation.
Peritonism from perforation is typically aggravated by any movement, not relieved by a posture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profuse bilious vomiting with inability to tolerate oral intake

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Profuse vomiting and secondary ileus are typical; inability to hydrate is the key driver of the ED referral.
Complete inability to tolerate oral intake signals dehydration requiring IV fluids.

symptoms.symptom_name: Scleral icterus (yellowing of the whites of the eyes)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Obstructive jaundice supports a biliary (gallstone) aetiology; caregiver/video-observable so admissible in telehealth, unverified.
Jaundice plus fever/rigors would indicate cholangitis — an immediate emergency.
Gallstones are the leading cause of acute pancreatitis in the absence of alcohol.
Suggests intravascular volume depletion from vomiting and third-space loss.

symptoms.symptom_name: Epigastric tenderness with voluntary guarding and absence of board-like rigidity

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Focal epigastric tenderness fits pancreatitis; the absence of board-like rigidity argues against a completed perforation — but this is a clinician exam the AI cannot perform.
No peritonism on exam makes a free perforation less likely — an in-person finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diminished bowel sounds with abdominal distension (paralytic ileus)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Secondary ileus indicates significant retroperitoneal inflammation — auscultation is clinician-only.
Objective markers of systemic inflammatory response / hypovolaemia; not obtainable without a device.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum lipase greater than three times the upper limit of normal

investigation_details.expected_result: elevated above the diagnostic threshold (raw value withheld — hospital laboratory result, not available via telehealth)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Diagnosis and severity cannot be confirmed; delay risks under-resuscitation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lipase >3x ULN with characteristic pain is diagnostic — but it is a pending hospital investigation, never available in real time by chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: No haematemesis and no melaena at presentation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No current bleeding — but its appearance is a hard 000 escalator, so it is screened and safety-netted.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Boring epigastric-to-back pain, postural relief on leaning forward, and profuse bilious vomiting together strongly point to acute pancreatitis.
Jaundice on a gallstone background with fever is the cholangitis pivot — escalate to 000 if rigors develop.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum lipase (and LFTs, FBC, U&E, CRP)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms pancreatitis and gauges severity and biliary involvement — hospital bloods, part of urgent ED assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Abdominal ultrasound (biliary tree) and CT abdomen if severe
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Identifies gallstones / common-bile-duct obstruction and excludes perforation and necrosis; imaging is in-hospital.
Excludes an atypical epigastric myocardial infarction before anchoring on a GI cause.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home / outpatient blood tests arranged by the telehealth AI
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Do NOT defer ED transfer to arrange outpatient tests — this is a same-day hospital presentation, not an outpatient work-up.
